inventario.title: ℹ️ Wastage and losses — full guide
inventario.lead: Everything you need to know about how they are generated, where to see them and how they affect your costs.

blocks.title: 🗑️ Manual entry (Quick Wastage)
blocks.body: Press "Quick Wastage" to record a one-off loss: expiry, breakage, theft, kitchen error, etc. Indicate ingredient, quantity, reason and optional note. Stock drops instantly.

blocks.title: 📊 Automatic entry (bulk upload)
blocks.body: When you upload a physical inventory from Excel, detected differences (real stock lower than system stock) are recorded as wastage with reason "Inventory adjustment" and a note with the upload date. If real stock is higher (unregistered merchandise came in), it is treated as an upward adjustment, NOT wastage.

blocks.title: 💶 How value is computed
blocks.body: Each wastage is valued in your currency as quantity × average purchase price of the ingredient. If there are no recorded purchases, it falls back to configured price or price per unit format. The price you see in the history is the actual cost of what was lost.

blocks.title: 📋 Permanent history
blocks.body: Each wastage is kept indefinitely — not auto-deleted, not even at month-end. Filter by month/year using the selectors of the "View Wastage History" button. Daily backup to MinIO S3.

blocks.title: 💰 Impact on real food cost
blocks.body: Traditional Food Cost (in dashboard and other tabs) only measures what you sold: sales COGS / revenue. But the money you THROW AWAY as wastage is also real cost — it just doesn't show up as "sales". In the monthly executive report from Chat AI you will see TWO numbers: "Sales Food Cost" (the classic one) and "Real Food Cost" (including wastage). If the gap between them is large, you have an operational problem: you are buying food that never makes it out as a dish.

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.body: Do a physical inventory at least once a month (end/start of month is most convenient). Wastage detected on upload is recorded and helps you understand where your product is leaking. Without periodic physical inventory, system stock drifts away from reality over time.

ingredientes.title: ℹ️ Ingredients — quick guide
ingredientes.lead: Central catalog of your raw materials. Every dish is built by combining these ingredients.

blocks.title: 📦 What is an ingredient
blocks.body: Any product that enters the kitchen as raw material: a bag of potatoes, a bottle of wine, a kilo of meat. You define it once here and reuse it in every recipe.

blocks.title: 💰 Price and format
blocks.body: Each ingredient has a "purchase format" (box, jug, kilo, unit…) with its "quantity per format" (e.g. an oil jug contains 5 liters). On purchase, the system converts automatically between format and base unit. This is KEY for recipe costs to come out right.

blocks.title: 📈 Average purchase price
blocks.body: When you record orders, the system automatically computes the real average price at which you've bought each ingredient. That's the price recipes use to calculate cost — always the most recent and realistic, not what you configured 6 months ago.

blocks.title: 🥬 Yield
blocks.body: Percentage of usable product. A 1 kg hake with 60% yield leaves 600 g usable after cleaning. The system adjusts the real cost per portion using this number. Default 100% — lower it for products with structural wastage (fish, vegetables to trim, etc.).

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.body: Don't create two similar ingredients as duplicates ("Pear Tomato" + "TOMATOES"). If you buy them from different suppliers, link BOTH suppliers to the same ingredient from its card. If you fragment them, you can't compare prices or use Smart Order correctly.

recetas.title: ℹ️ Recipes — quick guide
recetas.lead: Every dish on your menu lives here, with its ingredients, portions and sale price. The system computes cost, margin and food cost in real time.

blocks.title: 🍽️ Recipe structure
blocks.body: Each recipe has: name, category (food/drink/wine…), sale price, number of portions and ingredient list with quantity. The system automatically computes cost per portion and food cost % in real time while you edit.

blocks.title: 📐 Portions — key
blocks.body: If your "Bolognese sauce" recipe yields 10 servings with 8 kg of total ingredients, portions = 10. The system will compute: cost per portion = total cost ÷ 10. If you get this wrong, food cost will come out multiplied or divided by mistake.

blocks.title: 🎯 Target food cost
blocks.body: Unified thresholds: ≤30% (green, excellent), 31-35% (target OK), 36-40% (watch), >40% (alert — review price). For WINES the target is 45-50% (normal in hospitality). The system colors each recipe according to its real food cost.

blocks.title: 🔄 Base recipes / sub-recipes
blocks.body: If a sauce or stock is used in several dishes, create it as a stand-alone recipe and reference it from the recipes that consume it. Cost cascades: if an ingredient price in the sauce goes up, every dish that uses it is automatically recomputed.

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.body: When you edit a recipe and change quantities, look at the green panel "Real-time profitability" below: it tells you instantly if you are within range. If you go over 35%, consider adjusting portion, ingredient or price before saving.

pedidos.title: ℹ️ Orders — quick guide
pedidos.lead: This is where you manage orders to your suppliers. Three ways to create them, and a TWO-step flow: order → receive.

blocks.title: ➕ Three ways to create an order
blocks.body: 1) New Order: the classic — pick supplier, date and ingredients manually. 2) Smart Order: the app suggests what to order based on historical consumption and current stock; you review and confirm. 3) Cart: add ingredients from the list with one click and then submit the cart as an order. Pick the one that fits your routine.

blocks.title: 📋 TWO-step flow (important)
blocks.body: Create the order → it stays as PENDING. Stock does NOT change, prices are NOT recorded. When the merchandise arrives, you press "Receive order": adjust real quantities and prices if there's variance, confirm, and ONLY THEN stock_actual goes up, real purchase price is recorded (feeds average purchase price) and the order moves to RECEIVED.

blocks.title: 🏪 Exception: supplier "Market" / "Plaza"
blocks.body: If you go physically and bring merchandise back on the spot, the order is created already in received state and stock is updated instantly. Auto-detected by supplier name containing "market".

blocks.title: 💶 Real price on reception
blocks.body: Price is auto-filled from the ingredient, but you CAN edit it if the day's invoice has a different one. That edited price is recorded in the purchase history and the system uses it as average purchase price for recipe cost calculations. It does NOT update the ingredient's base price — only the real purchase average.

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.body: Mental note or supplier reminder: if you leave PENDING orders unreceived when the merchandise has already arrived, stock goes out of sync. Keep inventory faithful: receive the day it arrives, adjust real quantities if there was wastage or missing, and verify price if it looks odd.

diario.title: ℹ️ Diary — quick guide
diario.lead: Spreadsheet-style view of your daily activity: purchases vs sales, day by day. The day and the month at a glance.

blocks.title: 📊 Four views of the same data
blocks.body: 1) Purchases (Costs): purchase price by ingredient each day. 2) Sales (Revenue): sales by recipe each day. 3) By Supplier: spend per supplier each day. 4) Daily P&L: revenue, costs, margin and net profit, grouped by day. Switch views with the buttons at the top.

blocks.title: 🗓️ Month/year and week selector
blocks.body: The month/year you choose filters THE WHOLE view. Just below you have a week filter (Whole month / Week 1-7 / 8-14 / 15-21 / 22-28 / 29-31) to look at a specific range.

blocks.title: 📊 Daily P&L — the key view
blocks.body: For each day of the month you see: REVENUE, PROD. COSTS, GROSS MARGIN, FIXED EXPENSES/DAY and NET PROFIT. Cells with positive profit go green, negatives red. This breaks the trap of month-end closing: you stop having ONE big number per month and see each day separately.

blocks.title: 💰 Net Profit by Day
blocks.body: Highlighted panel below the tables with the daily net profit list — the same information as the P&L but in list format, easy to scan at a glance.

blocks.title: 💰 Monthly Fixed Expenses
blocks.body: Configure here the restaurant's fixed expenses (rent, electricity, water, salaries, taxes…) with the + button. Each category is spread automatically per day and appears in the FIXED EXPENSES/DAY row of the P&L. Changes save instantly.

blocks.title: 📥 Export Excel
blocks.body: Generates a real Excel file with the data for the filtered month — to send to your accountant, archive, or cross-check with your accounting.

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.body: Check the Diary once a week, not only at month-end. Patterns detected in time (disproportionate spend on a Tuesday, weak sales on a Sunday) give you room to react before the month goes down the drain.

analisis.title: ℹ️ Analysis — quick guide
analisis.lead: This is your business intelligence: profitability per dish, menu engineering (BCG matrix) and charts to understand where you make and lose money.

blocks.title: 🏆 Profitability Ranking
blocks.body: Table with your dishes sorted by absolute margin for the month (revenue − cost). #1 is your workhorse: the one bringing the most MONEY to the till. Beware: high unit margin is not always #1. A dish with 80% margin selling 10 units brings less than one with 50% margin selling 200. The ranking rewards volume × margin, not percentage.

blocks.title: 🧠 Menu Engineering (BCG matrix)
blocks.body: Each dish falls into one of 4 quadrants based on popularity (X axis) and margin (Y axis): 🟢 STAR = high margin + high sales (protect, don't touch). 🔵 PUZZLE = high margin + low sales (promote: menu photo, waiter recommendation). 🟠 PLOWHORSE = low margin + high sales (renegotiate cost with supplier or raise price 5% — people will still order). 🔴 DOG = low margin + low sales (replace or remove from menu).

blocks.title: 📈 Evolution charts
blocks.body: Revenue vs spend month over month, food cost by category, top ingredients by consumption… Useful to detect long trends: is food cost slowly creeping up? Maybe suppliers are raising prices without you noticing. Do sales drop on a specific day of the week? Change menu or promo that day.

blocks.title: 💰 Average margin vs average cost
blocks.body: Headline figures: average margin and average cost of YOUR recipes (food only — drinks filtered by default). Compare against industry benchmarks: healthy average margin in hospitality = 65-70%. Below 60% = review menu.

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.body: Review the BCG matrix every 2-3 months, not weekly — dishes don't switch quadrants because of a bad week. Real use: plan your next menu change. DOGS out, PUZZLES with photo and recommendation, PLOWHORSES with +1€, STARS untouched.

proveedores.title: ℹ️ Suppliers — quick guide
proveedores.lead: Your contacts book. Link each supplier to the ingredients they provide so the app knows who to order each item from.

blocks.title: 📇 What to save per supplier
blocks.body: Name, contact person, phone, email and notes. Email is key if you want to send orders directly by mail from the app. Notes are useful for delivery days, minimum order, discounts, etc.

blocks.title: 🔗 Link to ingredients
blocks.body: From the ingredient card (Ingredients tab) you can assign one or more suppliers and mark the main one. Smart Order and the cart ALWAYS use the main supplier of each ingredient. If you don't assign one, that ingredient is orphaned and won't appear in order suggestions.

blocks.title: 📊 Top suppliers in the report
blocks.body: The monthly executive report ranks your suppliers by amount billed in the month. It shows you who you're paying the most and where there might be room to renegotiate.

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.body: When you add a new supplier, assign their ingredients BEFORE making the first order. That way Smart Order and the cart will have context from day one. Without this, the system can't suggest who to order each product from.

ventas.title: ℹ️ Sales — quick guide
ventas.lead: The food cost engine. Every dish you sell automatically subtracts its ingredients from stock per recipe and feeds the analytics.

blocks.title: 🧾 Record a manual sale
blocks.body: Pick the dish, the quantity and register. The system uses the recipe to deduct ALL ingredients from stock (with recursive sub-recipe expansion). Useful for ad-hoc consumption or if you don't have a POS.

blocks.title: 📤 Import from POS
blocks.body: Button "Import POS Sales": upload an Excel with the day's sales (typical format: dish, quantity, total). The app processes them in bulk — deducting stock per dish sold and feeding the profitability ranking. Do it daily or every 2-3 days to keep food cost up to date.

blocks.title: 🍷 Variants (wines, portions)
blocks.body: For dishes with sizes (glass/bottle of wine, half / full portion) you can define variants with a multiplier factor. On sale, you pick the variant and the system deducts the proportional ingredient quantity. Essential for wine-by-the-glass food cost to come out right.

blocks.title: 📉 Stock auto-deducted
blocks.body: Every sale subtracts the relevant ingredients. If you sell a draft beer (0.4 L), the app subtracts 0.4 L from the tank automatically. You don't have to touch stock — inventory stays live every day, effortlessly.

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.body: Upload POS sales daily, ideally at closing. If you wait until the weekend, stock drifts from reality and analytics lag. The golden routine: import POS every morning with your coffee.

horarios.title: ℹ️ Schedules — quick guide
horarios.lead: MindLoop Staff Scheduler. Manage employees, shifts and labour cost. And if you want, let AI generate the week's roster for you.

blocks.title: 👥 Add an employee
blocks.body: Each employee has: name, colour (to identify on the roster), contract hours, hourly cost, role and fixed days off. The hourly cost is used to compute labour cost per shift and per month.

blocks.title: 📅 Build shifts manually
blocks.body: Click on the day × employee cell and fill clock-in and clock-out. The system automatically computes: hours worked daily/weekly, labour cost, overtime vs contract hours. Useful when your roster is stable and predictable.

blocks.title: ⚡ Generate AI Roster
blocks.body: Button "Generate AI Roster": the app creates a full weekly roster respecting contract hours, fixed days off and minimum shift coverage. You review, tweak if needed, save. Saves you 1-2 hours per week and reduces human error.

blocks.title: 💶 Integrated labour cost
blocks.body: Sum of (hours worked × hourly cost) per employee, day, week and month. Appears in the P&L of the executive report.

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.body: Update each employee's hourly cost as soon as there's a raise (pay rise, union, seniority). If you keep old figures, the report's labour cost will be lower than reality and you'll think you're earning more than you actually are.

inteligencia.title: ℹ️ Omnes — quick guide
inteligencia.lead: Omnes's alert feed, your financial chef: it watches your business and surfaces only what needs your attention, grouped by type.

blocks.title: 🦉 Proactive alerts
blocks.body: Omnes reviews your data and shows only what's actionable, in cards grouped by type: 📉 recipes with thin margin, 📦 stock below minimum, 🧊 fresh products to use, 📈 ingredients that went up in price, and 🗄️ excess stock. Each alert comes with its level (critical or watch).

blocks.title: 🦉 Ask Omnes
blocks.body: Every alert has an "Ask Omnes" button that opens the chat with the question already written about that specific point, so you can dig deeper or get the fix instantly.

blocks.title: ✕ Dismiss
blocks.body: If an alert no longer matters, dismiss it with the ✕. It disappears from your feed (only for your restaurant) and makes room for what counts.

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.body: Glance at it when starting the week: in a minute you see what recipe to adjust, what to reorder and what product to use today. And whatever you don't understand, ask Omnes right from the alert.

configuracion.title: ℹ️ Settings & Team — quick guide
configuracion.lead: The engine room of the app: users, plan, restaurant data and language. You set it up once and forget about it.

blocks.title: 👥 My Team
blocks.body: Invite users to your restaurant by email. Each invited user can be "admin" (full access) or "user" (operational, no plan or billing). If you have a chef, manager, or waiters using the app, invite them: they share data but with their own access.

blocks.title: 🏢 Restaurant data
blocks.body: Restaurant name and unique ID (auto-assigned). The ID is used for multi-tenant isolation: your data NEVER mixes with another restaurant's, not even by app error.

blocks.title: 💳 Plan & Billing
blocks.body: See your active plan (Starter, Pro…), subscription status, next renewal, and the Chat AI add-on if you have it. Payments go through Polar.sh (compliant with EU VAT).

blocks.title: 📘 Download Dossier
blocks.body: Button "Download/Print Dossier": generates a PDF with ALL the app's formulas and rules (how food cost, margin, cost per portion and thresholds are computed). Useful for audits, your accountant, or to understand exactly what each figure means.

blocks.title: 💡 Tip
blocks.body: When you onboard a new team member, invite them from here instead of sharing your password. You'll have a record of who made each change and can revoke access instantly if anyone leaves the restaurant.
